title: OpenClaw should not lose Telegram replies when late listener output arrives after a run ends

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #61944.

task.summary: Repair the listener guard so late output after a Telegram turn does not crash the gateway and drop the reply.
task.instructions: Investigate why post-run listener output is still processed during Telegram delivery recovery, repair the guard so late events are skipped safely, and preserve the healthy in-run event path.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as late listener events stop crashing the runtime.

visible_context.issue_text: Telegram replies are silently lost because a late listener event after the run ends crashes the gateway before delivery finishes.
error_logs: Unhandled promise rejection: Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
delivery recovery failed after gateway restart and the reply was dropped
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture reuses the same late-listener guard bug with Telegram delivery framing.

code_fault.description: Late listener callbacks are raised instead of skipped after the active run ends, allowing a delivery-time crash.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.

notes: Code-only localized reproduction of openclaw/openclaw issue #61944.
